tests/fixtures/material_ssh_fixture.openssh-v1 — reproduction note
==================================================================

This file is an Ed25519 OpenSSH v1 ASCII-armored private key, generated
once and committed verbatim. We do NOT regenerate at CI time because
key generation uses OS randomness — the same recipe produces a different
key each run.

The fixture exists for two purposes:

  1. Empirical byte-determinism guard test
     (src/payload/ingest.rs::ssh_tests::ssh_key_canonical_re_encode_is_byte_deterministic)
     — validates D-P7-11 on ssh-key 0.6.7's PrivateKey::from_openssh +
     to_openssh(LineEnding::LF) round-trip.
  2. Phase 7 Plan 08 round-trip / banner-render tests build on the same
     fixture — Plan 05 commits it; Plan 08 references it.

Recipe (requires OpenSSH client tools):

  ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "" -N "" -f /tmp/cipherpost_ssh_fixture_key

  cp /tmp/cipherpost_ssh_fixture_key tests/fixtures/material_ssh_fixture.openssh-v1

The key is unencrypted (-N "" → empty passphrase). Cipherpost's ingest
pipeline NEVER asks the user to encrypt material — it accepts whatever
OpenSSH v1 PrivateKey blob the user supplies, then encrypts the WHOLE
envelope under age (X25519, recipient-targeted). Encrypted-at-rest SSH
keys are out of scope for this milestone (D-P7-12 user-facing message
points users at `ssh-keygen -p -o -f <path>` for format conversion ONLY,
not encryption removal).

Algorithm: Ed25519 (the simplest OpenSSH v1 algorithm; no RSA modulus
parameterization, no EC curve choice — research D-P7-10 confirmed
ed25519 parsing works without the `ed25519` cargo feature on ssh-key
0.6.7 because the parse path uses `sha2` unconditionally).

File size: 387 bytes (small, fits comfortably under the 64 KB plaintext
cap with room for the age envelope overhead).

SHA-256 of the committed file (recorded at generation time):
  3cb6b44426bd6c004348a65d8c42b694e7256df6bbd3d361528c59025acf1e46  material_ssh_fixture.openssh-v1

Public key (for reference; the full pub line lives at the original
ssh-keygen output `tests/fixtures/material_ssh_fixture.openssh-v1.pub`
if regenerated, but we only commit the private half — Phase 7 Plan 06
preview rendering recovers the public half from the private file):
  ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIJFHrl4QVZLni+CoonIHDhIGt+ayrVNH7YjeAidxtlJE

Generated: 2026-04-24, OpenSSH ssh-keygen (system OpenSSH client).
